annotations.label: Experimental mathematics

anchor.snippet: Experimental mathematics is an approach to mathematics in which computation is used to investigate mathematical objects and identify properties and patterns.

annotations.note: A methodological/philosophical description of a research style, not a mathematical statement to be formalized.

annotations.label: Bailey–Borwein–Plouffe formula discovery

anchor.snippet: the discovery in 1995 of the Bailey–Borwein–Plouffe formula for the binary digits of

annotations.note: No BBP formula or hex-digit extraction for π is present in Mathlib (grep for `Bailey|Plouffe|BBP` returned nothing).

annotations.label: Counterexample to Euler's sum of powers conjecture

anchor.snippet: Roger Frye used experimental mathematics techniques to find the smallest counterexample to Euler's sum of powers conjecture

annotations.note: Mathlib has no statement of Euler's sum-of-powers conjecture nor Frye's counterexample.

annotations.label: ZetaGrid search for Riemann counterexample

anchor.snippet: The ZetaGrid project was set up to search for a counterexample to the Riemann hypothesis

annotations.note: The Riemann hypothesis itself is stated in Mathlib as `RiemannHypothesis`, but the computational search project is not.

annotations.label: Search for Collatz counterexample

anchor.snippet: searched for a counterexample to the Collatz conjecture

annotations.note: Grep for `Collatz`/`collatz` in `Mathlib/` returns no results.

annotations.label: Great Internet Mersenne Prime Search

anchor.snippet: The Great Internet Mersenne Prime Search is searching for new Mersenne primes

annotations.note: Mersenne numbers and the Lucas–Lehmer test are formalized as `mersenne` and `lucasLehmerResidue`, but GIMPS itself is a search project, not a theorem.

annotations.label: Great Periodic Path Hunt

anchor.snippet: The Great Periodic Path Hunt is searching for new periodic paths.

annotations.note: No formalization of this distributed-computing search project.

annotations.label: OGR project for optimal Golomb rulers

anchor.snippet: OGR project searched for optimal Golomb rulers

annotations.note: Grep for `Golomb` in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: PrimeGrid search

anchor.snippet: The PrimeGrid project is searching for the smallest Riesel and Sierpiński numbers.

annotations.note: Riesel and Sierpiński numbers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lorenz attractor discovery

anchor.snippet: Edward Lorenz found the Lorenz attractor , an early example of a chaotic dynamical system , by investigating anomalous behaviours in a numerical weather model.

annotations.note: Grep for `Lorenz` returns no results; the Lorenz system is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ulam spiral discovered by accident

anchor.snippet: The Ulam spiral was discovered by accident.

annotations.note: The only `Ulam` matches in Mathlib are Mazur–Ulam; the Ulam spiral is not present.

annotations.label: Ulam numbers pattern

anchor.snippet: The pattern in the Ulam numbers was discovered by accident.

annotations.note: Ulam numbers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Feigenbaum constant discovery

anchor.snippet: discovery of the Feigenbaum constant was based initially on numerical observations, followed by a rigorous proof.

annotations.note: Grep for `Feigenbaum` returns no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hales's proof of Kepler conjecture

anchor.snippet: Thomas Hales 's proof of the Kepler conjecture .

annotations.note: The Kepler conjecture is not in Mathlib (proved separately in HOL Light / Isabelle by Flyspeck).

annotations.label: Four colour theorem

anchor.snippet: Various proofs of the four colour theorem .

annotations.note: Grep for `four[_ ]?colo(u)?r` in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Non-existence of projective plane of order 10

anchor.snippet: Clement Lam 's proof of the non-existence of a finite projective plane of order 10.

annotations.note: Mathlib has projective planes but not Lam's computer-assisted non-existence result for order 10.

annotations.label: Minimum Sudoku requires 17 clues

anchor.snippet: Gary McGuire proved a minimum uniquely solvable Sudoku requires 17 clues.

annotations.note: Grep for `Sudoku` in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Hydrogen molecule-ion analytical solution

anchor.snippet: Solutions to a special case of the quantum three-body problem known as the hydrogen molecule-ion were found using standard quantum chemistry basis sets

annotations.note: A physics/quantum-chemistry result not in scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wheeler–Feynman potential equivalence

anchor.snippet: the equivalence between an advanced Liénard–Wiechert potential of particle j acting on particle i and the corresponding potential for particle i acting on particle j was demonstrated exhaustively

annotations.note: Liénard–Wiechert potentials are physics; not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Series expansion for ultrashort light pulses

anchor.snippet: verification of the series expansion of the envelope of the electric field for ultrashort light pulses travelling in non isotropic media

annotations.note: An optics computation not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Au-Yeung/Borwein identity

anchor.snippet: the following identity was rediscovered by Enrico Au-Yeung, a student of Jonathan Borwein using computer search and PSLQ algorithm in 1993

annotations.note: Neither the Au-Yeung sum identity nor the PSLQ algorithm appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Indra's Pearls visual investigations

anchor.snippet: David Mumford and others investigated various properties of Möbius transformation and the Schottky group using computer generated images

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Schottky group and no Kleinian-group results; Möbius transformations exist only via tangentially related material.

annotations.label: Plausible near-identity differing at 42nd decimal

anchor.snippet: The two sides of this expression actually differ after the 42nd decimal place; the left-hand side is smaller by approximately

annotations.note: This high-precision near-coincidence example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cyclotomic polynomial height failure at n=14235

anchor.snippet: the maximum height (maximum absolute value of coefficients) of all the factors of

annotations.note: Cyclotomic polynomials are formalized (e.g. `Polynomial.cyclotomic`), but the specific height-bound failure at n=14235 is not.
